Request a Demo

SAM4 Platform

Monitoring from the electrical cabinet. Across your entire motor fleet.

SAM4 captures electrical signals at the motor control cabinet, detects faults with AI, and validates every alert with reliability engineers. No sensors on the machine. No production downtime to install.

How SAM4 works

From electrical signal to maintenance action

1

Electrical signal

Current and voltage captured at the MCC via CT/VT clamps

2

Edge processing

Signal digitised and compressed on-site

3

AI detection

Cloud models identify anomalies across 15+ fault categories

4

Engineer validation

Samotics diagnostician confirms fault type, severity, and action

5

Maintenance action

Validated alert delivered to your team or CMMS

What you get

Four components. One managed service.

Sensor + comms hardware

Captures electrical signatures at the MCC. No sensors on machines.

Signal processing + AI analytics

ML models detect anomalies and classify fault types from electrical data.

Monitoring service

Every alert reviewed by a reliability engineer before reaching your team.

Dashboard + API

Fleet-wide interface for daily use and quarterly reporting.

Dashboard

Built for daily checks and quarterly reviews

Daily monitoring

  • Automated alerts ranked by severity
  • Anomaly tracking across assets
  • Fleet health at a glance
  • Per-asset energy consumption and efficiency

Quarterly reviews

  • Trend analysis and fleet reports
  • ROI tracking and cost avoidance
  • Energy savings identified and quantified
  • Asset prioritisation recommendations

Dashboard screenshot available on request. Request a live demo →

1200×600 SAM4 dashboard interface Fleet overview. Dark header with SAM4 logo. 6 asset cards in grid. Each shows status, name, type, health spark, timestamp. Summary: Total 247, Healthy 220, Warning 15, Critical 12.

Alert validation

Every alert is validated before it reaches your team

Signal
anomaly
→
AI
detection
→
Engineer
validation
→
Maintenance
recommendation

Diagnostics expertise

Reliability engineers with deep knowledge of pumps, compressors, fans, and motors. Every anomaly reviewed. Fault type confirmed.

Severity assessment

How far the fault has progressed. Degradation rate. Estimated time to failure. Not guesswork.

Recommended action

Specific maintenance steps written by the diagnostician. "Replace bearing within 4 weeks" not "something is wrong."

SAM4 is a managed service. Not self-serve software.

Behind the platform is a team that knows your fleet, your sites, and your maintenance priorities. Human-verified alerts. Actionable recommendations. No alert fatigue.

Diagnostic depth

What your team receives with every alert

Real alert example

Asset Pump P-301A
Fault Inner race bearing defect
Severity High
Lead time 4–6 weeks
Action Schedule bearing replacement at next planned outage

Not just an alert: a diagnosis

Every SAM4 alert names the specific fault type, rates its severity, and recommends the maintenance action. Your team knows what to do without opening a second system.

Weeks of lead time, not hours

ESA detects mechanical faults early in their progression. This alert gave the maintenance team 4–6 weeks to plan a bearing replacement. No emergency shutdown, no production loss.

Spectral signatures included

Alert cards include spectral signatures and trend graphs for full diagnostic context. Engineers can verify the finding, track degradation over time, and build confidence in the system.

Getting started

SAM4 deployment takes weeks, not months.

1

Asset selection

Identify critical assets where early detection has the highest impact.

2

Installation + onboarding

Hardware installs at the MCC in under 60 minutes. Dashboard live same day.

3

Training

Structured training for every user role. Your workflow, not ours.

4

Ongoing support

Named customer success manager. Quarterly reviews. Continuous improvement.

Integrations

From detection to maintenance action

SAM4 alerts reach your maintenance team where they already work. CMMS work orders, SCADA dashboards, or custom API endpoints. The detection-to-action loop closes automatically.

1

SAM4 detects

AI identifies an anomaly. A Samotics diagnostician validates the fault type, severity, and recommended action.

2

Alert flows to CMMS

Validated alert pushes directly into your CMMS as a notification or work order. SAP Plant Maintenance, IBM Maximo, or any system that accepts API input.

3

Your team acts

The work order lands in the existing maintenance workflow. No new system to learn. The fault information is where your team already works.

CMMS integration

SAP Plant Maintenance & IBM Maximo

Industry-standard CMMS platforms for heavy industrial maintenance. SAM4 integrates with both, plus any system that accepts REST API input.

SAP Plant Maintenance

SAM4 alerts create notifications or maintenance orders in SAP PM. Asset mapping links SAM4 assets to your SAP functional locations. Alert data populates the order description, priority, and recommended action.

Notification type, priority mapping, and field configuration are set during onboarding.

IBM Maximo

SAM4 creates work orders in Maximo with fault classification, asset reference, and diagnostic context. The integration maps SAM4 severity levels to Maximo priority codes.

Supports Maximo 7.6+ and Maximo Application Suite (MAS).

REST API & custom systems

Any CMMS that accepts REST API input can receive SAM4 alerts. The integration layer is standardised. Samotics configures the mapping between SAM4 alert fields and your system's work order schema.

Custom integrations typically configured within 1–2 weeks.

Want to see how we secure these integrations? View security & compliance →

Enterprise features

Built for multi-site, multi-team operations

SAM4 scales from a 20-asset pilot to a 10,000+ asset fleet. The enterprise features below exist because our largest customers demanded them.

Multi-site management

Manage assets across multiple sites, regions, and business units from a single dashboard. Filter, group, and compare by any dimension.

Role-based access

Configure visibility and permissions by role. Site engineers see their assets. Regional managers see their sites. The CTO sees the fleet. SSO integration available.

Integrations

SAM4 connects to your existing maintenance systems: SAP PM, IBM Maximo, and other CMMS platforms. Alerts and diagnostics flow directly into your work orders. Data exports and automated workflows available via REST API.

Leadership reporting

Pre-built reports for quarterly reviews, regulatory compliance, and internal stakeholder updates. Avoided downtime, energy savings, and maintenance cost impact, formatted for leadership. Export to PDF or share live links.

See how it installs → Security & compliance → Integration options →

“Samotics offers us an innovative perspective on our assets, unveiling insights into previously hidden situations. This newfound visibility lets us make informed, proactive decisions that significantly improve our maintenance efficiency.”
